preview_text:
The model favors Spain heavily, assigning an 90% win probability. Saudi Arabia's chance of victory registers at essentially zero in the model, while a draw carries a modest 11% probability. This reflects a forecast of a substantially uneven contest dominated by one side.

The win-draw-loss spread underscores Spain's strong expected advantage. The 79-percentage-point gap between Spain's win probability and Saudi Arabia's means the model sees little genuine two-way competition; instead it anticipates a likely Spanish victory, with a draw as the only realistic alternative outcome. A Saudi Arabia win barely features in the statistical projection.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely on a Spanish victory, differing by roughly 3 percentage points. Both assign similar draw probability around 10%. The market assigns 4% to a Saudi Arabia win, whereas the model yields zero—a meaningful gap that suggests bookmakers see slightly more upside risk for the underdog than the statistical forecast does. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate.
